item.title: Describing the UART protocol
item.description: UART (Universal Asynchronous Receiver Transmitter ) This protocol is asynchronous in the sense that the clocks between the 2 communicating devices are not in sync. So how is the communication achieved? This is done by using the real-time as an agreement for the transfers. What I mean with this is that even if one device has a clock of 100MHz and the other of 500MHz then they agree that in specific time slots there will be new data.

item.title: Testing a new page
item.description: Testing this page to see if it gets uploaded online

item.title: Showcase
item.description: Header 2 Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Nullam nec interdum metus. Aenean rutrum ligula sodales ex auctor, sed tempus dui mollis. Curabitur ipsum dui, aliquet nec commodo at, tristique eget ante. Donec quis dolor nec nunc mollis interdum vel in purus. Sed vitae leo scelerisque, sollicitudin elit sed, congue ante. In augue nisl, vestibulum commodo est a, tristique porttitor est. Proin laoreet iaculis ornare. Nullam ut neque quam.

item.title: The "figure" Shortcode
item.description: Hugo has figure shortcode built in, so you can easily add figcaptions or hyperlink rel attributes to images. Documentations can be found here: https://gohugo.io/content-management/shortcodes/#figure This theme has 3 CSS classes made for figure elements: big: images will break the width limit of main content area. left: images will float to the left. right: images will float to the right. If a figure has no class set, the image will behave just like a normal markdown image: !

item.title: Post With Featured Image
item.description: Just define the image URL in the content’s front matter, the featured image will be displayed as the background. For example: --- images: - https://picsum.photos/1024/768/?random --- This is an array, you can set multiple urls, only the first url will be used. These images is also used in Twitter Cards and the Open Graph metadata.

item.title: About Hugo
item.description: Hugo is the world’s fastest framework for building websites. It is written in Go. It makes use of a variety of open source projects including: https://github.com/russross/blackfriday https://github.com/alecthomas/chroma https://github.com/muesli/smartcrop https://github.com/spf13/cobra https://github.com/spf13/viper Learn more and contribute on GitHub.

item.title: About
item.description: Hello everyone!My name is Spyros Chiotakis, and like you, I’m just a regular person trying to find my path through life. I studied Computer Engineering in Greece at the Hellenic Mediterranean University at Heraklion, Crete. Then I received my masters at the University of York in Digital Systems Engineering at the United Kingdom. Currently working in France at Elsys Design as a microelectronics engineer. The blog’s purpose is to educate on topics such as, digital design, computer architecture, HDL languages (VHDL/Verilog), and if time allows, I will try to explain a few things about OS and compilers in correlation with computer architecture.
